claim: T regulatory cells (tTregs) lacking αvβ8 are unable to suppress pathogenic T-cell responses during active inflammation.

result.claim: T regulatory cells (tTregs) lacking αvβ8 are unable to suppress pathogenic T-cell responses during active inflammation.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper ID 17997584, titled "Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation". Proposition ID prop_3401 explicitly states: "Treg cells lacking expression of integrin αvβ8 were unable to suppress pathogenic T cell responses during active inflammation." The abstract of this paper further corroborates this, stating, "However, Treg cells lacking expression of integrin αvβ8 were unable to suppress pathogenic T cell responses during active inflammation." No contradictory evidence was found in the search. While the paper's year and citation count are unknown, the directness and clarity of the evidence are high.

evidence.text: Triggering of PD-1 expressed on monocytes by PD-L1 expressed on various cell types led to reversible CD4+ T cell dysfunction.
evidence.source: Programmed death-1–induced interleukin-10 production by monocytes impairs CD4+ T cell activation during HIV infection

evidence.text: Treg-cell-specific deletion of integrin αvβ8 did not result in a spontaneous inflammatory phenotype.
evidence.source: Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation

evidence.text: In mice, SNOs increase beta-AR expression and prevent agonist-stimulated receptor downregulation.
evidence.source: Regulation of β-Adrenergic Receptor Signaling by S-Nitrosylation of G-Protein-Coupled Receptor Kinase 2

evidence.text: CD44v6 expression was up-regulated in cell lines.
evidence.source: Regulation of CD44v6-containing Isoforms during Proliferation of Normal and Malignant Epithelial Cells

evidence.text: Treg cells lacking expression of integrin αvβ8 were unable to suppress pathogenic T cell responses during active inflammation.
evidence.source: Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation

evidence.text: GEF-H1 and ECT2 are essential for RhoA activation during cytokinesis.
evidence.source: Role of RhoA-specific guanine exchange factors in regulation of endomitosis in megakaryocytes.

evidence.text: KLF4 target genes regulate pro-inflammatory responses of SMCs.
evidence.source: KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of smooth muscle cells has a key role in atherosclerotic plaque pathogenesis

evidence.text: TGF-β-producing CD4(+) T cells played a protective role in F11r(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Compromised intestinal epithelial barrier induces adaptive immune compensation that protects from colitis.

evidence.text: In mice on a Western diet, genetically induced deficiency of mast cells reduces levels of inflammatory cytokines in serum.
evidence.source: Deficiency and pharmacological stabilization of mast cells reduce diet-induced obesity and diabetes in mice

evidence.text: Myeloid-derived suppressor cells (MDSC) suppress innate and adaptive immunity.
evidence.source: Tumor-Derived G-CSF Facilitates Neoplastic Growth through a Granulocytic Myeloid-Derived Suppressor Cell-Dependent Mechanism

evidence.text: TCR-CD3 engagement of human CD3 gamma-deficient T cell lines resulted in normal or slightly affected regulation of expression of several cell surface molecules including the TCR-CD3 itself.
evidence.source: Contribution of CD3g to TCR regulation and signaling in human mature T lymphocytes

evidence.text: mTOR signalling in cancer cells regulates G-CSF.
evidence.source: Oncogenic mTOR signaling recruits myeloid-derived suppressor cells to promote tumor initiation

evidence.text: Dendritic cells (DCs) play a critical role in the immune response to viral infection.
evidence.source: PQBP1 Is a Proximal Sensor of the cGAS-Dependent Innate Response to HIV-1

evidence.text: Regulatory T cells prevent eradication of tubercle bacilli by suppressing an otherwise efficient CD4+ T cell response.
evidence.source: Cutting Edge: Regulatory T cells prevent efficient clearance of Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

evidence.text: Effector regulatory T cells express high amounts of the integrin αvβ8.
evidence.source: Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation

evidence.text: The protective CD4+ T cell response was not correlated with increased numbers of IFN-gamma- or TNF-alpha-expressing cells in infected organs compared with wild-type C57BL/6 animals.
evidence.source: Cutting Edge: Regulatory T cells prevent efficient clearance of Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

evidence.text: Minor allele carriage limits inflammatory responses in monocytes via a FOXO3-driven pathway.
evidence.source: Human SNP Links Differential Outcomes in Inflammatory and Infectious Disease to a FOXO3-Regulated Pathway

evidence.text: Pediatric tissue T(reg) cells suppress endogenous T cell activation.
evidence.source: Early life compartmentalization of human T cell differentiation and regulatory function in mucosal and lymphoid tissues

evidence.text: PDS causes checkpoint activation in HR-defective cells.
evidence.source: Targeting BRCA1 and BRCA2 Deficiencies with G-Quadruplex-Interacting Compounds

evidence.text: PDS reduces proliferation of HR-defective cells.
